div.p: Architectural Spec: Two-level paging. Each process context (Task) maintains a unique 4KB Page Directory (PD).

ul.li: Context Isolation: While Process A and B use the same Virtual Addresses (e.g., 0x08048000), their CR3 registers point to different Physical PDs.
Memory Safety: Process A cannot generate a physical address pointing to Process B's user data because its Page Directory lacks any PTE pointers to those frames.
Shared Kernel: Both PDs contain identical pointers in the [768..1023] range, mapping the same physical kernel frames into every process address space.
